name: screenshot_generation
purpose: Generate a Playwright-based screenshot script for all app pages defined in the UI module specs. Patches configuration.json with a screenshots section and package.json with the required script entry and devDependency.

uiModuleSpecs.note: Exclude ui-catalog.json — only per-module specs (ui-{module}.json) contain the pages array

sampleData.note: Read to derive sampleIds for parameterized routes — use first record id from each JSON array file

configuration_patch.when: section screenshots is absent from configuration.json

auth.note: Credentials used to login before capturing each page. Override via env vars SCREENSHOT_EMAIL / SCREENSHOT_PASSWORD.

sampleIds.description: Map of parameterized page IDs (containing :id) to sample UUID values. AI derives these by reading output/sample-data/ files and matching entities to route context.
derivation_rules: For routes under /leave-types/:id — use first id from output/sample-data/leave-types.json
For routes under /employees/:id  — use first id from output/sample-data/employees.json
For all other :id routes (leave requests, team approvals, hr requests) — use first id from output/sample-data/leave-requests.json
Skip a parameterized page if no matching sample data file exists — log a warning in the generated script

package_json_patch.post_install_note: After patching package.json, print a reminder: run `npm install && npx playwright install chromium` before executing test:screenshots.

script_generation.runtime: Node.js (CommonJS require — no ESM, no TypeScript, no build step)
structure: Read configuration.json from project root using path.resolve(__dirname, '..', 'configuration.json')
Read screenshots.uiModulePattern and resolve it as a glob relative to project root — use the glob package
Exclude any file whose basename is ui-catalog.json
For each UI module spec file: iterate spec.pages[], skip parameterized pages with no entry in screenshots.sampleIds (warn to stdout), resolve :param segments using screenshots.sampleIds[page.id]
Build a flat array of { id, name, module, url, filename } — filename = page.id with slashes/colons replaced by hyphens, .png extension
Launch Playwright chromium headless at viewport 1440×900
Open one browser context and one page — login ONCE using screenshots.auth.email and screenshots.auth.password
Login flow: goto baseUrl/login → fill email input → fill password input → click submit button → waitForURL not containing /login
For each app page: goto url, waitForNetworkIdle, waitForTimeout 800ms, take fullPage screenshot, write PNG to outputDir/filename
Print summary: N captured, N failed. Exit code 1 if any failures.
error_handling: If a page fails (navigation error, timeout) — log [fail] pageName: error.message, increment failed counter, continue
If login fails — abort with a clear message: 'Login failed — is the app running at {baseUrl}?'
If no pages are found — abort with: 'No pages found. Check screenshots.uiModulePattern in configuration.json.'

rules: CRITICAL: The script must be pure CommonJS (require/module.exports) — no import/export syntax, no TypeScript.
CRITICAL: Use the glob npm package (v10 sync API or glob.sync) to resolve uiModulePattern — do not use fs.readdirSync manually.
CRITICAL: Login once per run using a shared browser context — do NOT open a new context per page.
CRITICAL: screenshots.sampleIds keys must exactly match page.id values from the UI module spec (e.g. '/my-leave/requests/:id'), not router path strings.
CRITICAL: Patch package.json by reading and merging — do NOT overwrite the entire file.
CRITICAL: Patch configuration.json by reading and merging the screenshots section — do NOT overwrite existing keys.
Generate the script so it is runnable immediately after npm install — no compilation required.
outputDir from configuration.json is relative to project root — resolve it with path.resolve(ROOT, outputDir) in the script.
The script must create outputDir recursively with fs.mkdirSync if it does not exist.
Print per-page [ok] / [fail] / [skip] lines to stdout for visibility during CI runs.
Do not hardcode any URLs, credentials, or IDs in the script — all values must come from configuration.json or environment variable overrides.
